title: Components · The Unit of Reuse
scan: What you will learn: A component is a self-contained UI unit with four architectural dimensions — State, Events, Rendering, and Configuration
Why it matters: The presentational vs container split is the single most impactful structural decision at component design time — it determines testability, reusability, and AI-delegation safety
Components that mix presentation with data-fetching cannot be tested in isolation, reused across contexts, or safely delegated to an AI agent
Configuration is the escape valve that keeps a component reusable — anything that varies between placements belongs in config, not code

blocks.html: odels represent entities. Components represent self-contained pieces of the UI — and in modern frontend development they are both the unit of reuse and the unit of contract between teams.

DEPT® field pattern: define the component's config schema (TypeScript interface or JSON Schema) before writing any logic. The schema is the contract; the code is the implementation. A component whose config schema cannot be written in 10 minutes has not been designed yet.

Components with more than one network call in their lifecycle are almost always container components written as presentational ones. The symptom: the component is not reusable because it is hardwired to a specific API endpoint. Extract the data layer into a container.

A well-formed component is, in miniature, what Stalwart enforces at feature scale: it owns its state, declares its events, encapsulates its rendering, and accepts configuration from outside. That is why component thinking translates so cleanly into AI-generated code — each component is a tiny Stalwart.

cards.title: State
cards.body: What the component remembers between interactions — open/closed, selected value, loading.

cards.title: Events
cards.body: What it emits when something happens — change, submit, select. Its public action surface.

cards.title: Rendering
cards.body: How it draws itself given its state and props. The visual contract.

cards.title: Configuration
cards.body: Props in, slots/children supplied. How the parent customises it.

columns.label: Presentational
columns.body: Stateless or near-stateless. Props in, DOM out. A Button, a Card, a Badge. The most reusable layer of a design system.

columns.label: Container
columns.body: Holds state, talks to APIs, coordinates child components. A SearchBox, a Dashboard, a CheckoutFlow. Closer to a feature than a primitive.

blocks.html: The split matters: presentational components are shared across features; containers are features themselves. The Stalwart file structure (CODER's C) is essentially the anatomy of a container component grown to feature scale.

blocks.label: Architect's Review · Components
items: Is there a component inventory, with owners?
Are component contracts (props, events, slots) documented?
Is the presentational vs container split clear in the codebase?
What's the test strategy per component type?
Is there a shared component library, and who governs it?
